element_introduction_order.mastery_context: foundational combat in initial ship sections
defines starting skill loadout and playstyle trajectory
required for accessing alternate areas and discounted vending machines
continuous skill progression system throughout game
resource management at vending machines for ammo/health

element_introduction_order.first_appears: available from start (OSA branch) or acquired

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: primary antagonist force; outbreak explained via audio logs
anti-personnel vs armor-piercing vs energy rounds for specific foes
maintenance requirement for sustained combat effectiveness
death checkpoint system at cost of 10 nanites per use
distinct enemy type requiring energy weapons or armor-piercing ammo
bypass hacking minigame; resource scarcity drives choice

typical_relief_triggers: successful hack or lock pick use
skill upgrade completion at cyber-upgrade unit
audio log discovery providing narrative clarity
typical_tension_triggers: weapon degradation without repair tools available
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: asymmetric exploration-combat cycle with resource attrition tension; narrative tension built through progressive audio log revelation of catastrophic outbreak

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: alternate areas behind hackable doors; ICE-pick bypasses signal hidden paths
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: audio logs and apparitions hidden in exploration spaces reward thorough investigation

difficulty_curve.early_game: foundational combat and system introduction with manageable threat level; career choice establishes difficulty trajectory (Marine=combat-focused, Navy=hacking-dependent, OSA=psionic exploration)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: ammunition scarcity intensifies resource management; weapon degradation becomes critical constraint; multiple enemy types require ammunition specialization knowledge
difficulty_curve.late_game: exotic alien weaponry becomes viable but requires sustained nanite investment; cyber upgrades reach diminishing returns; narrative escalation through audio logs and apparitions
notable_spikes: first major genetic enemy wave forcing adaptation from starting loadout
introduction of mixed enemy types requiring ammunition type switching
late-game resource bottlenecks if early nanite spending was inefficient

notable_level_patterns.description: Ammunition and health packs become progressively harder to locate; player forced toward careful ammo selection and weapon degradation management; hacking skill becomes more valuable for vending machine price reduction

Backstory explained through audio log acquisition during exploration rather than cutscenes; ghostly apparitions serve as both environmental storytelling and atmosphere building; encourages thorough room searching

Genetic infected emerge early as primary threat; protocol droids and mechanical foes introduced later requiring alternate ammunition strategies; progression teaches players to recognize and counter distinct threat profiles
Early hacking minigame success unlocks resource discounts and alternate routes; ICE-picks provide high-value optional bypass but are scarce; Navy career enables easier access to these benefits
notable_level_patterns.example_level: any section with vending machines or secured doors
